# 🌐 Multilingual Text Classification

This repository contains the code and resources for the paper **"Multilingual Text Classification: A Comprehensive Survey"**.

## 📄 Paper

You can access the paper here: [Multilingual Text Classification: A Comprehensive Survey](https://arxiv.org/abs/2409.13316)

## 📚 Abstract

Text classification is a fundamental task in natural language processing (NLP) that involves assigning predefined categories to text data. With the increasing amount of multilingual content on the internet, there is a growing need for text classification models that can handle multiple languages effectively. This paper provides a comprehensive survey of multilingual text classification, covering various aspects such as datasets, preprocessing techniques, feature extraction methods, classification algorithms, and evaluation metrics. We discuss the challenges and opportunities in multilingual text classification and highlight recent advances in the field. Additionally, we present a comparative analysis of different approaches and provide insights into their strengths and limitations. This survey aims to serve as a valuable resource for researchers and practitioners working on multilingual text classification tasks.

## 📂 Repository Structure

- `data/`: Contains datasets used in the study.
- `src/`: Source code for preprocessing, feature extraction, and classification.
- `models/`: Pre-trained models and model checkpoints.
- `notebooks/`: Jupyter notebooks for experiments and analysis.
- `results/`: Results and evaluation metrics.

## 🚀 Getting Started

To get started with the code, follow these steps:

1. Clone the repository:
    ```bash
    git clone https://github.com/yourusername/multilingual-text-classification.git
    ```
2. Navigate to the project directory:
    ```bash
    cd multilingual-text-classification
    ```
3. Install the required dependencies:
    ```bash
    pip install -r requirements.txt
    ```
4. Run the preprocessing script:
    ```bash
    python src/preprocess.py
    ```
5. Train the classification model:
    ```bash
    python src/train.py
    ```
6. Evaluate the model:
    ```bash
    python src/evaluate.py
    ```

## 📊 Datasets

The following datasets are used in this study:

- [Dataset 1](link-to-dataset-1)
- [Dataset 2](link-to-dataset-2)
- [Dataset 3](link-to-dataset-3)

## 📈 Results

The results of our experiments are summarized in the table below:

| Model          | Accuracy | F1 Score |
|----------------|----------|----------|
| Model 1        | 0.85     | 0.84     |
| Model 2        | 0.87     | 0.86     |
| Model 3        | 0.88     | 0.87     |

## 🤝 Contributing

We welcome contributions to this project. Please read the [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) file for guidelines on how to contribute.

## 📜 License

This project is licensed under the MIT License - see the [LICENSE](LICENSE) file for details.

## 📧 Contact

For any questions or suggestions, please contact us at [your-email@example.com](mailto:your-email@example.com).
